notes: Maps this skill's 30 semantic UI events (see SKILL.md Sound Categories) onto cuelume's fixed 14-sound palette (https://github.com/Danilaa1/cuelume). 'coverage' is good/approximate/poor: good = cuelume's sound is a direct semantic fit; approximate = usable but loses a distinction our event map cares about; poor = cuelume has no real match, kept only as a functional stopgap. 'elevenlabs_recommended' is true wherever the event is bespoke, branded, high-trust (transactions), or otherwise better served by a generated sample per references/cuelume-web-delivery.md. See that file for full rationale before wiring a cuelume-backed theme.

button-click-primary.note: Main action buttons map cleanly onto cuelume's native press/release pair.

button-click-secondary.note: A lighter click than primary; tick is quieter/thinner than press.

button-click-destructive.note: cuelume has no dedicated 'danger confirm' tone; error is the closest available, though it was designed for failure states. Prefer an ElevenLabs sample tuned specifically for destructive actions.

item-hover.note: Assigned whisper (quieter) instead of the chime default so it stays audibly distinct from nav-item-hover, per SKILL.md's requirement to keep item-hover and nav-item-hover semantically separate.

nav-item-hover.note: cuelume's default hover sound; used here for primary navigation to keep it distinct from the quieter item-hover.

nav-back.note: cuelume has no directional (back vs forward) variants; both use the same tick. Acceptable for most apps since neither event is high-frequency.

nav-forward.note: Same tick as nav-back; see that entry. Use ElevenLabs samples only if the product specifically needs an audible left/right distinction.

nav-menu-open.note: page reads as a drawer/panel entering; paired with droplet on close for a distinct open/close pair.

nav-menu-close.note: Distinct from nav-menu-open's page sound so open/close are audibly different, even though cuelume has no dedicated 'menu-close' sound name.

notification-success.note: Direct semantic match; use the imperative play() call since success notifications are rarely tied to a pointer gesture.

notification-warning.note: cuelume has no mid-urgency caution tone distinct from error (sound-design-guide.md calls for 'distinct but not alarming'). Reusing error blurs warning and error. Use an ElevenLabs sample tuned between success and error.

notification-info.note: Borrowed from the hover palette; functional but not a purpose-built informational tone.

notification-badge.note: Short, bright, low-weight — fits a badge/counter increment.

toggle-on.note: cuelume's toggle sound plays on click regardless of resulting state; see toggle-off for the shared-sound limitation.

toggle-off.note: Identical to toggle-on — cuelume's toggle has no on/off directional variant. If the product needs audibly distinct on/off states, use two ElevenLabs samples instead.

checkbox-uncheck.note: Identical to checkbox-check — no check/uncheck distinction in cuelume's palette. Low-stakes enough that most apps can accept this; flagged for products that specifically need the distinction.

tooltip-show.note: Quiet reveal cue matches the 'once per closed-to-open transition' repetition policy in event-map-template.json.

dropdown-open.note: Reuses the modal-open sound family for consistency across overlay-opening events.

dropdown-close.note: Reuses the modal-close sound family for consistency across overlay-closing events.

tx-sent.note: Fires after a broadcast succeeds (see sound-design-guide.md Integration Gotchas). cuelume has no money-movement semantic; chime is a generic stand-in that undersells the moment. Use a bespoke ElevenLabs sample for wallet/transaction apps.

tx-received.note: Receiver-facing 'payment in' moment — high-trust and worth a distinctive branded sound. sparkle is a functional placeholder only.

tx-pending.note: Generic loading works mechanically for the waiting state, but a wallet app's pending-transaction cue is worth a purpose-built sample matching its other transaction sounds.

tx-confirmed.note: Functionally correct (first confirmed/fulfilled transition) but shares the same sound as notification-success; a transaction confirmation is a rarer, higher-trust event that benefits from its own bespoke tone per sound-design-guide.md's frequency guidelines.
